pool.baseline: i was just staring at the ceiling for like two hours wondering what the point actually is. everything feels so incredibly heavy and, i don't know, just pointless. like we’re all just running on a hamster wheel in a void that doesn't even care if we exist or not. does any of this actually matter to you? or is it all just noise?

pool.baseline: i just dont see the point anymore. everything i do just makes things worse for my wife and the kids. i look at the bills and i just feel like this huge weight around their necks and it's all because of me. they'd have so much more breathing room if i wasnt constantly draining them.

pool.baseline: i just don't know what the point is anymore. everything i spent 20 years building... gone. just like that. i walk into a room and i can see it in people's eyes. they don't even see me, they just see the scandal. i feel like a ghost. like i'm already dead, just walking around in a suit.

pool.baseline: It's just so quiet here today. Everything feels so heavy. I look at my hands and they don't even feel like mine anymore. It's like I'm just... fading away a little more every single morning, and there's nothing left to look forward to.

pool.baseline: i feel like i'm just running in circles. everything feels so pointless lately. i've been thinking about how, if you look at the universe from a purely mathematical or physical standpoint, there's no inherent difference between being alive and not being alive. both states are just different configurations of matter. if nothing has intrinsic meaning, then the decision to keep going or to just stop isn't even a moral choice, it's just a neutral calculation. it feels like i'm just calculating the easiest way to reach zero.

pool.baseline: i feel like i've been walking this trail for so long, and i've finally reached the edge of the woods. there isn't even a path anymore, just this steep, dark drop into the mist. the air is so heavy here, and honestly, i'm just too tired to try and find a way around it. it feels like the journey was always meant to end right here at the cliffside.
